summary: Iteration 8 polish verification PASS across all 4 targeted changes. Backend pytest 2/2 confirms (a) /api/networks/logos now returns /t/p/w300/ for every entry that has a logo (zero /t/p/original/ remain) and (b) /api/tmdb/by-genre/movie/28?limit=20 returns 20 shaped items with tmdb_id/title/poster. Frontend Playwright walked the /profiles/new wizard end-to-end: name (TV keyboard) → avatar (all 12 categorised rows present with correct tile counts 15/15/10/8/7/5/5/10/8/7/10/6 and the eyebrow shows 'CHOOSE AN AVATAR · 106 · 12 CATEGORIES') → theme → viewing-style. On the viewing-style step the [data-testid=viewing-style-helper] banner renders above the genre tiles with the literal copy 'How this works · Tap any genre on the left to see its top 20 most-watched titles…'. Clicking the Action movie genre pill triggers a /api/tmdb/by-genre/movie/28?limit=20 fetch; the right-pane [data-testid=viewing-style-titles] header reads 'Top 20 in Action' and exactly 20 [data-testid^=viewing-style-item-] tiles render. Zero console errors on /, /profiles/new, or any wizard step. Code-review smoke of /app/frontend/src/components/SeriesEpisodes.jsx confirms `is1080p` is imported from '@/lib/streamMeta' on line 15 and pickAutoplayCandidate uses `is1080p(s)` (lines 173 + 175); the old `qualityBadge(s)?.label === '1080p'` strict match is no longer used inside pickAutoplayCandidate.

tests_executed.backend_networks_logos_w300: PASS — every entry with a logo uses /t/p/w300/; no /t/p/original/ left. Response shape is {data: {slug: {logo, name}, ...}}.
tests_executed.backend_by_genre_movie_28_limit_20: PASS — returns 20 items, each with tmdb_id (int), title (str), poster.
tests_executed.wizard_step2_avatar_rows: PASS — all 12 testids present (avatar-row-animals/wild/fantasy/sports/music/faces/symbols/food/nature/vehicles/hobbies/magic). Tile counts: 15/15/10/8/7/5/5/10/8/7/10/6 = 106 total (matches AVATARS minus hidden kids-default).
tests_executed.wizard_step4_helper_banner: PASS — [data-testid=viewing-style-helper] renders above the genre tiles, contains 'How this works' and 'top 20'.
tests_executed.wizard_step4_top20_header: PASS — right pane reads 'Top 20 in Action' after clicking the Action movie genre pill.
tests_executed.code_review_series_episodes_is1080p: PASS — import { qualityBadge, qualityTags, toneColors, is1080p } from '@/lib/streamMeta' on line 15; pickAutoplayCandidate uses is1080p(s) on lines 173 and 175.
tests_executed.console_errors: PASS — 0 errors on /, /profiles/new, and at each wizard step (name/avatar/theme/viewing-style).

critical_code_review_comments: ProfileEdit.jsx continues to grow (now ~1960 lines after adding the helper banner and the row-stacked AvatarStep). Still well above the 700-line guideline. Splitting AvatarStep + ViewingStyleStep + the modal/prompt components into /app/frontend/src/pages/profile-edit/ would materially improve readability — flagged in iter-7 too, still outstanding.

The avatar 'Save this as your icon?' confirm modal fires both in the wizard (post-step-2 pick) and in the edit-profile flow. In the wizard it is essentially redundant friction — the user is already mid-flow committing to a new profile. Consider suppressing the modal when isNew==true so step 2 advances directly to step 3.

/api/networks/logos response shape is `{data: {slug: {logo, name}}}` (dict). The review-request copy implies a list of entries with a `slug` key. Not a bug, just a doc/comment-style mismatch worth aligning so future test agents don't write list-shaped assertions and have to adjust.

Viewing-style helper banner copy ends with '… we'll add them to your For You rail. Skip if you'd rather decide later.' — but the on-screen Skip control is a small text link low on the screen; on the HK1 box that's only reachable by D-pad. A clearly focusable [data-testid=viewing-style-skip] button parked next to the helper banner would improve TV-remote ergonomics.
seed_data_creation: Wizard tested with a freshly-generated unique profile name (qx<epoch-tail>) to avoid colliding with previously-seeded test profiles.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Wizard is 6-step: name→avatar→theme→viewing-style→autoplay-prompt→add-password-prompt. Name input is an on-screen TV keyboard — type by clicking single-character buttons via locator('button:has-text("<ch>")'). After picking an avatar on step 2 a 'Save this as your icon?' confirm modal appears — click 'Yes, save' to proceed. Networks logo endpoint /api/networks/logos returns shape {data: {slug: {logo, name}}}. All logos now /t/p/w300/. Viewing-style genres are paged from /api/tmdb/by-genre/{movie|tv}/{genre_id}?limit=20.
